steps.title: Read the intercepts
steps.content: The graph has $y$-intercept $(0,3)$ and $x$-intercept $(-1.5,0)$. A line through those intercepts has positive slope.

steps.title: Build the equation
steps.content: Using the intercepts, the slope is $\frac{3-0}{0-(-1.5)}=2$, so the line is $y=2x+3$. Rewrite it: $$y=2x+3 \Rightarrow -2x+y=3$$ Multiplying by $6$ gives $$-12x+6y=18$$

steps.title: Match the positive constant
steps.content: The equation $-12x+6y=18$ has the form $-12x+Ry=18$ with $R=6$, a positive constant. That matches choice D.
